creator: wforms@Safe-mail.net
text:
I saved the output of dmesg under OpenBSD 4.2 (booting from the OpenBSD install CD). The "otherwise disappearing" optical drives nicely show up in OpenBSD's dmesg. I post it here as it might be helpful to identify our problem.

This is how my old PIII/800 desktop shows up:
<include>
OpenBSD 4.2 (RAMDISK_CD) #468: Tue Aug 28 11:02:17 MDT 2007
    deraadt@i386.openbsd.org:/usr/src/sys/arch/i386/compile/RAMDISK_CD

avail mem = 771678208 (735MB)

cpu0 at mainbus0

cd0(pciide0:1:0): using PIO mode 0, Ultra-DMA mode 4
cd1(pciide0:1:1): using PIO mode 4, DMA mode 2
uhci0 at pci0 dev 7 function 2 "VIA VT83C572 USB" rev 0x11: irq 12
"VIA VT82C596 Power" rev 0x30 at pci0 dev 7 function 3 not configured
rl0 at pci0 dev 8 function 0 "Realtek 8139" rev 0x10: irq 11, address 00:40:f4:64:4d:d5

"Creative AWE64 PnP, CTL0022, , WaveTable" at isapnp0 port 0x620/4 not configured

dkcsum: wd0 matches BIOS drive 0x80
root on rd0a swap on rd0b dump on rd0b
</include>


And this is how the Netfinity 5000 shows up:
